channel.title: ClaudeSpy Updates
channel.description: Most recent changes with links to updates.

item.title: Version 1.6
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy v1.6 Release Notes

Features

  • Project Search — Added a search field to project selection views, making it easier to find projects when you have many Claude Code sessions.
  • New Session Button — An empty sidebar now shows a "New Session" button, providing a clearer path to get started.
  • Resize Pane to Fit — New toolbar button to resize the tmux pane to match the mirror view dimensions, eliminating layout mismatches.
  • LSP Tools Support — Language Server Protocol tools now work correctly within monitored sessions.

Improvements

  • App Renamed to Gallager — The macOS app has been renamed from ClaudeSpy to Gallager.
  • Unlimited Reconnection — The Mac app now retries server connections indefinitely instead of giving up after 10 attempts, improving reliability for long-running sessions.
  • Terminal Cleared on Session End — The remote terminal view on iOS now clears when a session ends, preventing stale output from lingering.
  • Service Architecture — Migrated 8 internal services to a dependency injection pattern, improving testability and maintainability.
  • Unpairing Notifications — Other services are now properly notified when a device is unpaired.
  • E2E Testing Infrastructure — Added screenshot comparison, test report generation, Claude session hook scenarios, and improved test isolation from the production app.

Bug Fixes

  • Duplicate View Menu — Fixed an issue where the macOS menu bar displayed two "View" menus.
  • Keychain Updates — Resolved a bug where keychain values were not being updated correctly.
  • E2E Test Reliability — Fixed tmux socket permission errors and window detection issues in the end-to-end test suite.

item.title: Version 1.5
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.5 Release Notes

Features

  • Clickable Links in Terminal Mirrors — URLs displayed in mirrored terminal panes are now highlighted and clickable, making it easier to open links directly from monitored sessions.
  • E2E Test Coordinator with Storage Isolation — A new end-to-end test coordinator enables reliable integration testing with fully isolated storage, improving confidence in the test suite.
  • Signing Credentials Transfer for Multi-Machine Builds — A new script allows transferring code signing credentials between machines, simplifying setup for developers building on multiple Macs.

Improvements

  • Dependency Injection with PointFree Dependencies — Adopted the PointFree Dependencies library for structured dependency injection, improving testability and modularity across the codebase.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed Signing Certificate Selection — The transfer-signing process now correctly filters certificates by team and searches the Xcode provisioning profile directory, resolving issues where the wrong certificate could be selected during builds.

item.title: Version 1.4
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.4 Release Notes

New Features

Mac-to-Mac Viewer Mode

Monitor Claude Code sessions running on remote Macs directly from another Mac. Includes full terminal streaming, a dedicated pairing flow, and viewer UI — extending ClaudeSpy beyond mobile monitoring.

Session Creation from Sidebar

Quickly start new Claude Code sessions directly from the macOS and iOS sidebars, with support for both local and remote hosts.

Multi-Device Pairing

Pair multiple iOS devices with a single Mac app, so your whole team can monitor sessions simultaneously.

Launch at Login

Optionally start ClaudeSpy automatically when you log in, with a first-launch prompt to configure the setting.

Additional Hook Types

Added support for `TeammateIdle`, `TaskCompleted`, and other previously missing hook event types, enabling richer session monitoring and notifications.

Improvements

  • Dynamic port allocation for hook server — Multiple users on the same machine no longer conflict over ports.
  • Sessions grouped by Claude status — The macOS sidebar now organizes sessions by their current status for easier scanning.
  • Username included in Mac-to-iOS data — iOS devices now display which user a session belongs to.
  • Terminal view shown by default — iOS Claude sessions open directly to the terminal view instead of requiring an extra tap.
  • Improved terminal sizing — New sessions on Mac start with better default terminal dimensions.
  • Landscape layout refinements on iOS — Hidden tab bar and reduced navigation bar give more screen space in landscape orientation.
  • Updated SwiftTerm to v1.10.1 — Incorporates upstream terminal emulation fixes.
  • Refactored pairing protocol — Internal improvements to pairing response handling for better reliability.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed Cmd+V paste on macOS terminal — Paste now works reliably in the built-in terminal.
  • Fixed Cmd+Left/Right word movement — These shortcuts now move the cursor by word instead of typing "b" or "f".
  • Fixed menu bar window activation — Clicking the menu bar icon now reliably brings windows to the front.
  • Fixed push notifications not opening sessions — Tapping a notification now correctly navigates to the relevant session.
  • Fixed terminal reconnection getting stuck — Stream reconnections no longer hang on "connecting" indefinitely.
  • Fixed session sync on Mac reconnect — Sessions now sync correctly when the Mac reconnects while iOS is already connected.
  • Fixed iOS new session popover — The popover for creating sessions on the first paired Mac no longer opens blank.
  • Fixed high CPU usage — Resolved an issue that could cause excessive CPU consumption.
  • Fixed iOS compilation — Resolved a build issue affecting the iOS target.

item.title: Version 1.1
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.1 Release Notes

Features

Interactive Terminal Input (macOS)

Terminal mirrors on macOS now support keyboard input, allowing you to interact directly with monitored Claude Code sessions rather than just observing them.

Multi-Mac Server Support (iOS)

The iOS app can now connect to and monitor multiple Mac servers simultaneously, making it easier to keep track of Claude Code sessions running across different machines.

Plugin Installation Guide

First-time users on macOS will now see clear instructions for installing the Claude Code plugin, streamlining the initial setup process.

Bug Fixes

Improved Connection Reliability (macOS)

Fixed an issue where the Mac app would disconnect from the server and fail to automatically reconnect. The app now properly retries connections when they are interrupted.

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
What's New in 1.0

  • Initial release with Sparkle auto-update support
